topic.what: Legal insufficiency of post-hoc mitigation for training-stage copyright infringement
topic.how: Argues liability attaches at moment of copying/ingestion; outputs cannot retroactively cure completed acts; model weights constitute fixed infringing copies
topic.why_matters: Current industry reliance on post-hoc techniques (unlearning, guardrails) creates massive legal exposure including algorithmic disgorgement and financial penalties; shift to ex-ante compliance required

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether post-hoc technical interventions can cure training-stage infringement
characteristics.vs_baseline: Output equivalence assumption fails as remedy
characteristics.evidence: Completed-act doctrine: liability attaches at t=0 when copying occurs
characteristics.context: Legal doctrine analysis across US, EU, Japan jurisdictions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether protected expression persists in weights post-mitigation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Post-filtration models still embody infringing copies
characteristics.evidence: Weights satisfy fixation requirement; expression recoverable via forensic analysis or extraction attacks
characteristics.context: 17 U.S.C. §101 analysis: perceptibility via machine aids

characteristics.what_it_measures: Effectiveness of copyright fair use against contract/tort claims
characteristics.vs_baseline: Contract and tort override copyright exceptions
characteristics.evidence: ProCD v Zeidenberg: licenses stricter than copyright; Tsubasa System v Toppan: unfair competition applies
characteristics.context: Global encirclement of non-copyright liability paths

characteristics.what_itasures: Whether remedies extend to model itself vs only source data
characteristics.vs_baseline: Deleting source data does not neutralize head-start advantage
characteristics.evidence: In re Everalbum (FTC 2021); head-start doctrine: competitive advantage persists in parameters
characteristics.context: Unjust enrichment doctrine requires stripping all wrongful gains

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether AI training qualifies as transformative fair use
characteristics.vs_baseline: Warhol v Goldsmith: no transformation if commercial purpose shared and market substitute
characteristics.evidence: Generative AI competes for same user attention as training data; Sega complementarity requirement violated
characteristics.context: Post-Warhol fair use analysis for AI training

novelty: Completed-act doctrine applied to AI training liability
Binary Beacon mandatory opt-in regime proposal
open_problems: Transaction cost reduction for licensing billions of sources
Technical feasibility of ZKP-based negative verification
